blurb:
Twenty pounds, one year old, and named Crunchy. That alone deserves a second look.

Crunchy is a young Shepherd mix with a lot of energy and a genuinely easy-going personality. The shelter says he adapts well to new environments, and for a dog who just landed in a shelter at barely a year old, that says something real about his temperament. He's friendly with other dogs, vaccinated, neutered, microchipped, and already started on heartworm prevention.

His housetraining status is unknown, which is pretty normal for a young dog coming out of a shelter situation. He hasn't been around kids before, so homes with very young children should talk to the shelter first and see if a slow introduction makes sense. For a dog-friendly household with some patience and a routine, he's going to figure things out fast.

At 20 pounds and one year old, he's got some growing and learning to do. But dogs who adapt easily and get along with others tend to be the ones who settle in quickly once they land somewhere stable. Crunchy just needs that somewhere.

He's at Lewisville Animal Services on College Pkwy. Walk in during visiting hours to meet him.
shelter_hours: Contact shelter for current visiting hours
scoring_notes:
• The name alone earns clicks, and a 20-pound adaptable Shepherd mix at one year old is a broadly appealing adoption candidate
• Easy temperament in a shelter environment is a credible, standout signal that resonates with first-time dog owners
• Pet ID and blurb framing suggest a moderate stay with no extreme urgency, but youth and adaptability keep appeal high

blurb:
His name is Hypnotic, he's a two-year-old Belgian Malinois mix, and the shelter described him as a "leggy dude" who loves his people. That tells you a lot right there.

Hypnotic is housebroken, heartworm negative, vaccinated, neutered, and microchipped. He's got high energy, which tracks for a young Malinois, and he's fully devoted to the humans in his life. The busy-bee activity level on his profile is not a warning, it's a selling point for the right person.

With other dogs, he's not unfriendly, just particular. He wants a proper introduction before jumping into a friendship. That's not a red flag, that's just a dog with some social self-awareness. If you have another dog at home, a meet and greet at the shelter before adoption is the move. They can set that up.

His experience with kids is unknown since he came in without that history, so families with very young children should have that conversation with the shelter directly. But for an active household that can match his energy and give him a job to do, Hypnotic is going to thrive.

He's at Lewisville Animal Services on College Pkwy. Walk in during visiting hours or call ahead to arrange a meet and greet.
shelter_hours: Contact shelter for current visiting hours
scoring_notes:
• High-drive Belgian Malinois mix with a memorable name and devoted personality is a compelling match for the active-reader demographic
• Unknown kid history and selective dog socialization add honest nuance that builds shelter credibility without killing adoption interest
• Mid-range pet ID suggests moderate shelter tenure, lending enough urgency to justify feature placement in the newsletter

blurb:
Seven years old, litterbox trained, lived with other animals before, and just landed at the shelter. Carly is a new arrival and the staff is still getting to know her, but what they already have is a grown cat with a proven track record of coexisting peacefully with other pets.

Seven is a great age for a cat. She's not going to redecorate your furniture or run laps at 3am for the fun of it. She knows what she is. She just needs a household that knows what they want, and if what they want is a calm, settled cat who's been around the block, she's worth meeting.

She's vaccinated, microchipped, spayed, and up to date on flea treatment. The logistics are handled. Her full personality is still unfolding at the shelter, so the best way to get a read on her is to go say hi in person.

Lewisville Animal Services is on College Pkwy in Lewisville. Walk in during visiting hours.
shelter_hours: Visit during shelter visiting hours (call to confirm current schedule)
scoring_notes:
• Seven-year-old with proven multi-pet coexistence speaks directly to experienced cat owners seeking a settled companion
• Fully vaccinated, microchipped, spayed, and flea-treated removes every logistical hesitation at adoption time
• Also flagged as a new arrival, limiting time-based urgency despite strong overall placement potential

blurb:
The name alone is worth the trip. Darth Vader is a three-year-old black domestic shorthair, and yes, someone at the shelter has a great sense of humor. He weighs 10 pounds, which is just enough to make his presence known when he decides to sit on you.

The shelter says he does best with older kids, which probably just means he's got a little dignity and doesn't love being chased around the living room by a toddler. Honestly, fair. He's vaccinated, microchipped, and already neutered, so the logistics are handled.

They don't have a ton on his background yet, but a calm three-year-old cat with a litterbox habit already locked in is not a hard sell. If you've got a quieter household and you've always wanted a cat with a little mystique, this guy's available.

Lewisville Animal Services is on College Pkwy in Lewisville. Walk in during visiting hours to meet him.
shelter_hours: Visit during shelter visiting hours (call to confirm current schedule)
scoring_notes:
• Name-driven virality gives this blurb instant newsletter headline potential with zero effort
• Fully vetted, calm, and litterbox-trained adult cat removes every common adoption barrier
• Appears to be a recent intake, reducing urgency but not appeal for quieter-household readers

blurb:
Cody went through an adoption, got returned after two months, went through an immersive Prison Pups training program, and came out the other side as a dog the volunteers describe as a strong, lovable guy with a sweet nature. He's three years old, about 33 lbs, a Cattle Dog mix with a lot going on in the best possible way.

He was returned not because anything was wrong with him but because his first family couldn't keep up with his energy. That's honest, and it matters. He needs someone who actually wants a busy, playful, active dog. He went to doggie daycare in his temporary home and loved it. He's friendly with other dogs and does well playing alongside them. He calmed right down during his shelter bath when the pets and attention started. There's a lot of good dog in there.

He's been a bit mouthy during play, which is pretty normal for Cattle Dog mixes who are still learning. He's strong, so toddlers aren't a great fit, but he did well with kids in his temporary home. Cats are unknown. If you've got an active lifestyle, a dog-friendly household, and some time to keep him moving and mentally busy, Cody is going to thrive.

Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw. Head to their website to submit an inquiry or give them a call to meet Cody in person.
shelter_address: 2880 Kennesaw Due West Rd NW, Kennesaw, GA 30152
shelter_hours: Check mostlymutts.org for current adoption hours
scoring_notes:
• Return-and-redemption arc with honest energy disclosure builds reader trust and targets the right adopter effectively
• Prison Pups graduate with daycare success and strong volunteer endorsement adds credibility and warmth to the narrative
• Two placements and a training program stay indicate substantial time in the system — a timely feature could be the difference for Cody

blurb:
During his shelter bath, Wilson drank water straight from the sprayer and handed out kisses the entire time. That's pretty much all you need to know about this guy.

Wilson is a three-year-old Bulldog/Boxer mix, about 46 lbs, currently living in a foster home while he waits for the right family. He went through the Mostly Mutts Prison Pups immersive training program and came out knowing a lot more than he went in with. The volunteers actually use him to help other dogs learn how to play because he's that good with them. He's met kids of various ages in his foster home and handled it calmly and affectionately every time.

He's vocal when he plays, which is worth knowing upfront. He "talks" while he's having fun, a kind of happy chatter that's just how he expresses himself. If that sounds fine to you and you've got a dog at home who matches that energy, Wilson would probably love having a buddy. He does have a prey drive toward small animals on walks, so cats aren't the right fit. No toddlers either since he's strong and enthusiastic and could accidentally knock a small kid over. But for an active household that wants a goofy, affectionate, genuinely happy dog, he's the real deal.

Wilson is at Mostly Mutts in Kennesaw. Visit their website to submit an inquiry or call to set up a meet.
shelter_address: 2880 Kennesaw Due West Rd NW, Kennesaw, GA 30152
shelter_hours: Check mostlymutts.org for current adoption hours
scoring_notes:
• The sprayer-bath opener is immediately shareable and sets a joyful tone that will resonate across a broad readership
• Prison Pups program credential plus peer-teaching role gives Wilson a standout identity that no other candidate matches
• Foster placement and immersive training history suggest a meaningful wait — enough time in the system to warrant a featured spotlight

blurb:
She came back to the shelter a different dog than the one who left. Cecelia, a three-year-old Terrier/Boxer mix around 37 lbs, was adopted from Mostly Mutts and returned after nine months. When she came back, she was shut down and scared. The volunteers say she was barely recognizable. Now she's in a foster home, settling in, and doing great.

Here's who she actually is once she trusts you: a full-body-wag, crawl-into-your-lap dog who thinks she belongs on the couch with you. She's in the Mostly Mutts University training program and has already learned sit, stay, come, and leave it. Her foster family calls her the perfect housemate. The "high energy" label from her previous home? Doesn't match who she is now.

She does need the right setup. She'd do best as your only dog at home, since she tends to guard her person around other dogs in close quarters. She's still working through some wariness around new people, especially men, so she needs a patient owner who'll let her come to them on her own terms rather than reaching over her or rushing her. Mostly Mutts recommends kids 15 and older given her history. Give her time and space, and she gives you everything.

Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw. Submit an inquiry through their website or call to get the process started and ask about Cecelia.
shelter_address: 2880 Kennesaw Due West Rd NW, Kennesaw, GA 30152
shelter_hours: Check mostlymutts.org for current adoption hours
scoring_notes:
• Return adoption + emotional shutdown + full recovery is one of the most compelling rescue narratives available — readers will feel invested
• Specific placement requirements (only dog, older kids, patient owner) narrow the audience but make the right match deeply meaningful — strong call-to-action angle
• Nine-month adoption, return, and ongoing foster stay signal a meaningful time investment — urgency and emotional stakes are both high

blurb:
Cannoli came in from animal control a little while back, and he's still in that early shelter phase where everything is new and a little overwhelming. He's shy right now. Not scared exactly, just cautious. He's been warming up to the volunteers, starting to accept pets and cuddles, and the people at Mostly Mutts are putting in the time to help him get comfortable.

He's a tuxedo shorthair, about 2.5 months old, already neutered, microchipped, and current on his kitten shots. He does great with his siblings, which tells you he's going to be fine once he settles in. If you've got a patient household and maybe another gentle cat already at home, he'd probably thrive. The shyness isn't permanent. It just takes a little time and consistency, and then you've got a cat who genuinely trusts you because you earned it.

Cannoli is at the Mostly Mutts shelter in Kennesaw. You can stop by to meet him in person. Adoption fee is $175.
shelter_hours: Please check mostlymutts.org for current hours
scoring_notes:
• Shy disposition narrows the adopter pool, making newsletter visibility especially valuable for this kitten
• Backstory is thin — animal control intake with few distinguishing details limits emotional hook
• Recently arrived with no signals of extended wait, so urgency is low relative to other candidates

blurb:
Calico kittens are already hard to pass up, and then you add a name like Strudel and it gets even harder. She's about 2.5 months old, came in from animal control with her siblings, and is still getting her footing at the shelter. She's on the shy side right now, same as the rest of her litter, but the volunteers are working with her and she's already starting to come around to petting and attention.

She plays with her siblings, keeps her area clean, and is already spayed, microchipped, and up to date on her kitten shots. That's a lot done before she even hits three months. She does well with other cats and would probably love having a feline buddy in her new home. Gentle kids are welcome to come meet her. Dogs are an unknown.

The shyness is worth mentioning because it's real, but it's also completely normal for a kitten who's been through a transition this big this young. Give her a quiet home with some patience and she's going to open up fast.

Strudel is at Mostly Mutts in Kennesaw. Walk in to meet her. Adoption fee is $175.
shelter_hours: Please check mostlymutts.org for current hours
scoring_notes:
• Calico coloring plus a charming name is a proven reader-attention combination that drives clicks and inquiries
• Sibling litter context adds warmth but the story lacks a unique hook to stand apart from Cannoli
• Early in her shelter stay with no urgency signals, though shyness could extend her wait if not spotlighted soon

blurb:
She was born in someone's garage to a feral mom, weighed about two pounds, and spent her first weeks at the shelter fighting off an upper respiratory infection. That's a rough start for a 2.5-month-old kitten. But Souffle is doing better now, the sniffles are fading, and her personality is coming back online in a big way.

She's affectionate and playful, litter trained, and already rediscovering all the things kittens are supposed to be doing: chasing toys, pouncing on shadows, and investigating anything that looks remotely interesting. She's a tuxedo medium hair, so she's going to be gorgeous on top of all that. When she's done wearing herself out, she curls up close and purrs. Pretty solid trade.

Souffle does well with other kittens and would probably love a cat companion at home. Dogs and young kids are unknown territory, but gentle kids who know how to handle a small animal are welcome to come meet her. She's living in the Kitty Room at the shelter right now, getting stronger every day and waiting for her turn.

Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw. Stop by the shelter to meet her in person. Adoption fee is $175.
shelter_hours: Please check mostlymutts.org for current hours
scoring_notes:
• Tuxedo kitten with a recovery arc readers will root for — high emotional pull for newsletter audiences
• Feral birth + illness overcome = built-in underdog story without heavy baggage
• Recently arrived and recovering, so urgency is moderate but growing — timing is good to feature now

blurb:
Cody has a straightforward story. He came to Mostly Mutts from animal control, got adopted, and two months later his family brought him back. Not because anything went wrong, really. They just couldn't keep up with him. Cody is a 33-pound Cattle Dog mix who needs more activity than a lot of households can give him, and his previous family was honest about that. So now he's back, completed the Mostly Mutts Prison Pups training program, and is waiting for someone whose energy actually matches his.

The volunteers describe him as "a strong and lovable guy with a sweet nature." He's been to doggie daycare and loved it. He plays well with other dogs, though he can get a little mouthy during play, which is pretty normal for the Cattle Dog side of him. He calmed right down for his shelter bath and soaked up every pet and scratch. He just needs somewhere to put all that energy.

If you run, hike, have a yard, or already have an active dog at home who wants a buddy to keep up with, Cody is worth meeting. He's not the right fit for a low-key household, and that's okay. He knows that about himself too. Toddlers aren't the best match since he's strong and moves fast, but he did well with older kids in his temporary home.

Cody is at Mostly Mutts in Kennesaw. Head to their website to start the process.
shelter_hours: Please check mostlymutts.org for current hours
scoring_notes:
• Returned-for-lifestyle-mismatch story is relatable and honest — positions him as a great dog in need of the right fit, not a problem dog
• Prison Pups trained with daycare experience; well-socialized and structured, lowering perceived risk for active adopters
• Two-time shelter resident whose high energy needs limit the adopter pool, creating real urgency for the right active household

blurb:
Wilson went through the Mostly Mutts Prison Pups training program, came out knowing a lot, and is now living in a foster home waiting for someone to notice him. He's a 46-pound Bulldog-Boxer mix who is, by all accounts, just a genuinely happy guy.

He loves other dogs so much that his trainer actually uses him to help other dogs learn how to play. That's not a small thing. He's also vocal about it. Wilson will "talk" while he plays, which is just him letting you know he's having a blast. During his shelter bath, he drank water straight from the sprayer and gave out kisses the whole time. That's Wilson. He's met kids of various ages in his foster home and done well with all of them, calm and affectionate when kids come up to pet him.

A couple of things to know: no cats. He's shown a prey drive toward small animals on walks, so that's a firm no. And while he was friendly with the neighbor's chihuahua in foster, the general rule stands. He's also a strong, active dog, so toddlers who could get bumped over aren't the best fit. But if you've got older kids and maybe another dog at home who can keep up with an enthusiastic playmate, Wilson is going to fit right in.

He's in foster care now. Reach out to Mostly Mutts in Kennesaw to set up a meet.
shelter_hours: Please check mostlymutts.org for current hours
scoring_notes:
• Prison Pups graduate who now helps train other dogs — a credential that signals reliability and creates instant reader trust
• Bathtime sprayer story is a genuinely fun, humanizing detail that makes him memorable in a crowded field
• Foster placement without a match yet suggests a meaningful wait; his no-cats requirement narrows the pool and adds quiet urgency

blurb:
She's been through a rough stretch. Cecelia was adopted from Mostly Mutts, returned nine months later, and came back completely shut down and scared. Different dog than she was before. But she's been in a foster home since then, and she's doing great. Turns out she just needed someone to slow down and give her time.

When Cecelia trusts you, she does this whole-body wag thing. Not just the tail. Her whole body. And she will absolutely try to sit in your lap despite being a 37-pound terrier-boxer mix who clearly does not understand her own size. She's enrolled in Mostly Mutts University and has already nailed sit, stay, come, and leave it. Her foster family calls her the perfect housemate. The "high energy" label from her previous home? Doesn't match what they're seeing at all.

A few honest notes: She needs to be your only dog at home. She tends to guard her person around other dogs, and that's just who she is. She also needs time to warm up to new people, especially men, so don't reach over her head or rush her to come to you. Let her set the pace. Mostly Mutts recommends calmer kids 15 and older. If you have the patience to let her come to you on her terms, she will absolutely get there.

Cecelia is in foster care right now, so reach out to Mostly Mutts to set up a meet. Their shelter is in Kennesaw off Jiles Road.
shelter_hours: Please check mostlymutts.org for current hours
scoring_notes:
• Return adoption story with a documented emotional recovery arc is the most compelling narrative in this batch
• The whole-body wag detail and lap-dog-who-doesn't-know-her-size are vivid, shareable moments for newsletter readers
• Returned after nine months, came back shut down, and still waiting — the urgency and patience required make her ideal for a spotlight feature

blurb:
Strudel is a 2.5-month-old calico girl who came into Mostly Mutts through animal control and is still in the early days of figuring out shelter life. She's a little shy right now, which is about as expected as it gets for a kitten who just landed somewhere new. The volunteers are spending time with her, and she's already starting to come around on the whole petting-and-cuddles situation.

She does great with her siblings and is going to do great with a resident cat too. Put her somewhere with another friendly cat and she'll have a buddy to play with while she gains confidence with the people in the house. That combination tends to work really well for kittens like her. She's litter trained, up to date on shots, microchipped, and already spayed.

Adoption fee is $175. She's at the Mostly Mutts shelter in Kennesaw. If you've been thinking about adding a second cat and want one who's going to grow into the role at her own pace, Strudel is a solid candidate. Go meet her.

Mostly Mutts Animal Rescue
1100 Kennesaw Ave, Kennesaw, GA 30144
(770) 422-0992 | info@mostlymutts.org
Check mostlymutts.org for current hours
scoring_notes:
• Rare calico girl — visually distinctive coat type that reliably drives reader interest and sharing
• Fully spayed, microchipped, and vaccinated with no behavioral red flags beyond expected shyness
• Story is thinner than peers; urgency is modest, making her a supporting feature rather than a lead

blurb:
Cannoli came in through animal control a few weeks ago, and he's still figuring out that the humans at Mostly Mutts are actually on his side. He's a 2.5-month-old tuxedo boy, about 2 pounds, and a little shy right now. Not hiding-under-the-bed shy. More like cautious-new-kid-at-school shy. The volunteers are already working on it.

He loves playing with his siblings and is starting to warm up to people too. Petting and cuddles are getting a better reception every day. Once he settles in somewhere permanent and figures out that this is his place now, he's going to open right up. That's just how it goes with kittens who had a rocky early start.

He gets along great with other cats, so a house that already has a friendly resident cat could actually speed up his confidence a lot. He's litter trained, up to date on shots, microchipped, and already neutered. Adoption fee is $175. He's at the Mostly Mutts shelter in Kennesaw — stop in and ask to meet him.

Mostly Mutts Animal Rescue
1100 Kennesaw Ave, Kennesaw, GA 30144
(770) 422-0992 | info@mostlymutts.org

scoring_notes:
• Fully vetted — neutered, microchipped, vaccinated — lowering the barrier to a quick adoption decision
• Shy-but-warming arc gives readers the satisfying narrative of being the one who wins him over
• Recently arrived with a few weeks in shelter; modest urgency but consistent progress signals readiness

blurb:
A tuxedo kitten born in someone's garage to a feral mom doesn't exactly get the smoothest start in life. But Souffle, a 2.5-month-old medium hair girl, has been making up for it ever since. She landed at Mostly Mutts, fought through an upper respiratory infection with a lot of help from the shelter staff, and came out the other side ready to cause some kitten chaos.

She's litter trained, affectionate, and now that she's feeling better, she's back to doing full kitten things: chasing toys, pouncing on shadows, and investigating anything that looks even slightly interesting. She's also still very much into cuddle time. This is not a cat who will ignore you.

Souffle does well with other kittens and cats, so if you've got a resident cat who could use a companion, she's worth a look. Dogs and kids are unknown quantities, but gentle, older kids would probably be fine. She's living in the Kitty Room at Mostly Mutts in Kennesaw and is ready to meet you. Adoption fee is $175.

Mostly Mutts Animal Rescue
1100 Kennesaw Ave, Kennesaw, GA 30144
(770) 422-0992 | info@mostlymutts.org
Check mostlymutts.org for current hours
scoring_notes:
• Overcame illness and rebounded with full kitten energy — a natural feel-good adoption story
• Affectionate, litter trained, and cat-friendly makes her broadly appealing to a wide adopter pool
• Recently arrived kitten with low urgency signals, but health recovery arc adds emotional hook
